message.content: @startuml skinparam sequenceMessageAlign center autonumber "[0]" actor "sidetree-core-go" as User1 participant "Instance 1\nOperation Queue" as Instance1_OQ database "orb.operation\ndatabase" as DB { } rectangle "op-queue\nDB" as OPQueue_DB participant "Instance 2\nOperation Queue" as Instance2_OQ User1 ->> Instance1_OQ : Add(op1) activate Instance1_OQ Instance1_OQ ->> DB : Publish(op1) activate DB return ok deactivate DB return ok deactivate Instance1_OQ User1 ->> Instance2_OQ : Add(op2) activate Instance2_OQ Instance2_OQ ->> DB : Publish(op2) activate DB return ok deactivate DB return ok db --> Instance1_OQ : op2 activate Instance1_OQ db <- - Instance1_OQ :ok instance1_oq->OPQUEUE_db:Put(op2,handler1ID,expiry)\n(visibility timeout)\nop queue stores operation in Inflight state \nto prevent other instances from processing this message.\nThe visibility_timeout value specifies how long to wait before making the item visible again if not acknowledged by handler. note right of user1 #lightyellow:Note:\nsidtree core go has two handlers:\nhandler1 for handling operations on sidetree blockchain,\nhandling means creating batches and writing them into batch files store;\ni.e., adding new blocks/transactions onto chain; or updating existing ones based upon changes made since last update cycle ran successfully without errors occurring during execution time period specified above). Handler processes messages asynchronously using goroutines so there may be delays between receiving request/response pairs due network latency issues etc.. Also keep mind about potential race conditions where multiple requests arrive simultaneously causing conflicts over shared resources like memory allocation/deallocation routines used throughout entire application stack including third party libraries imported along way too! So always test thoroughly under various scenarios involving high load situations especially those related concurrency control mechanisms employed here such locks semaphores mutexes atomic variables
